content: We can conceptualize a two-layer self-improvement system for autonomous AI agents where Self-Refine acts as the iterative output polishing mechanism for immediate tasks, and Reflexion acts as the long-term memory-backed self-reflective layer that accumulates patterns from past Self-Refine cycles to update the agent's core heuristic for new tasks. This layered structure eliminates the need for both external supervision and static reward modeling across short- and long-term task horizons.
